description: Immediately recognizable.  I first noticed the rich brown chest above the black belly, then the gray face with a line of black features from the crown down the back of the back of the neck.  All four birds had distinctly orange bills.  Looking closer I could clearly see the greyish white tip of the bill.  Two of the birds alternately stood and sat on the grass (the other two remained sitting).  When they stood I could see the pink legs.  Although their wings remained folded the whole time, the white patch between the brown (above) and black (below) was easily visible.  I didn't think of focusing on the white eye-ring at the time, but in retrospect believe that their eyes stood out distinctly from the gray face.  Very striking and unmistakable for anything else; I knew what I was looking at right away, as I had seen Black-bellied Whistling-Ducks in Texas and in Florida.
